name: Analisis cruzado de fev_agent y fev_kanban
description: Extrae funciones publicas de fev_agent.prg y fev_kanban.prg, las combina en un documento comparativo en docs/fev_agent_kanvas.md

tasks.prompt: Read fev_agent.prg completely. List every function (Static Function, FUNCTION, and STATIC FUNCTION with its header comment description). For each, output: FUNCTION_NAME | LINE | DESCRIPTION. Include all functions from the header comment section (lines 4-55) plus any additional functions found in the body. Also identify which functions are called by or related to fev_kanban (look for references to Kanban, AgentDone, task, plan). Return the structured list as plain text.

Read fev_kanban.prg completely. List every function with its starting line number and description from the header comment. Output: FUNCTION_NAME | LINE | DESCRIPTION. Include all functions from the header (lines 4-30). Additionally, for each function, note which static variables it accesses and which fev_agent functions it calls (e.g. AICallFromKanban calls AIAsyncRun, AIThreadBody, etc.). Return the structured list as plain text.

Combine the results from task {{task.1-1.result}} (fev_agent functions) and task {{task.2-1.result}} (fev_kanban functions). Create a Markdown document at docs/fev_agent_kanvas.md with these sections: 1) Title '# fev_agent.prg <-> fev_kanban.prg — Cross-Module Analysis'; 2) 'Module: fev_agent.prg' — table of all functions with columns Function, Line, Description; 3) 'Module: fev_kanban.prg' — same table format; 4) 'Cross-Module Relationships' — list of connection points: which kanban functions call which agent functions, shared static variables (e.g. aOpenAgents, lAIRunning), and the data flow (Kanban task -> AICallFromKanban -> AIAsyncRun -> AgentDone -> KanbanMarkTaskDone). Ensure the docs/ directory exists before writing.
